domain: cosmochemistry (chondrule-matrix complementarity, primitive meteorite fractional condensation)
specific_mechanism: Chondrules (rounded silicate spheres) and the fine-grained matrix that hosts them in primitive chondrites are chemically *complementary*: volatile-poor chondrules pair with volatile-rich matrix at exactly the bulk-solar elemental ratio, indicating both components condensed from the same nebula gas reservoir under fractional, non-equilibrium thermal cycling. Each cycle attenuates the previous fractionation step, and complementarity emerges as the attractor of repeated condensation-evaporation cycles.
llm_application: Pretraining mixture as nebula-fractional-condensation: split corpus into two complementary streams — a 'chondrule' stream of high-density structured / volatile-poor documents (code, math, formulas) and a 'matrix' stream of high-volume / volatile-rich documents (web text, conversations, opinions). Train under repeated mini-curriculum cycles that alternate between the two; chemistry-style complementarity is the requirement that the union of the two streams matches the bulk-corpus elemental composition (token distribution). Feedback-attenuation: each curriculum cycle is shorter than the last, so deviations from the bulk distribution self-attenuate, with the asymptote being the complementarity attractor.
novelty_claim: Optimizing pre-training data mixtures (2025.acl-long.1564), Complementary-MoA ensembles (NeurIPS 2025 133207), HetCCL heterogeneous GPU training (2601.22585), and the 2025 LLM research-summary list — none specifically frames pretraining as fractional-condensation cycles producing chondrule-matrix-style complementarity at a bulk-token attractor.
